researcher_notes: Scope: YouTube channels with companion text-blog AND vertical-niche specialist sites (Arduino-only, ESP32-only, RPi-only, robotics-only, 3D-printing-only, PCB-only). Tested ~80 URLs across ~50 distinct domains. Key disqualifier: companion sites that publish ONLY show notes / 'watch the video' stubs without textual tutorial content — those are useless for a text-blog ingest pipeline. Sources where the feed parses but the body is video-promo only are explicitly flagged in `notes` and qualifies=false. Sources already confirmed in prior scopes (adafruit-learn, random-nerd-tutorials, makezine) are excluded. Also excluded duplicates with anglo.json: circuit-digest, engineersgarage, hackaday, instructables.

sources.name: How To Mechatronics
sample_titles_recent: DIY 3D Printed CNC Cuts Metal with 0.05mm Accuracy — Complete Build | HTMCNC
NERF Minigun Turret for the 3D Printed RC Tank
What is THE BEST 3D Printed Drive for Your Next Robotic Project
Creality K2 Plus Review — One of the Best 3D Printers
sources.notes: Dejan Nedelkovski's YouTube companion blog. Confirmed full textual tutorials (~175KB body on sample article) — wiring diagrams, code, BOM, step-by-step. Heavy on Arduino, CNC, 3D printing, robotics. Cadence is slow (monthly) but quality is top-tier. Most tutorials map directly to MechatronicStore catalog (Arduino, motors, stepper drivers, sensors).

sources.name: Educ8s.tv
sample_titles_recent: ESP32 S3 Web Radio Project
ESP32Cam Tutorial — Upload photos to your Server
ESP32Cam Tutorial — Build a Simple Camera
Waveshare esp32s3 1.91 AMOLED Review
ESP32 ESP-NOW Tutorial
sources.notes: Nick Koumaris's YouTube channel companion. ESP32/ESP32-CAM/Arduino tutorials with FULL code, schematics, wiring inline (sample article ~1.1MB body — embedded gist code blocks). The blog DOES include 'watch the video below' phrases but the textual content is also fully complete (code + step-by-step). Cadence dropped after 2025 — newest entry Sep 2025, total 10 items in feed. Still valuable as deep-archive ESP32 reference.

sources.name: Robot Zero One
sample_titles_recent: Raspberry Pi Zero Voice Controller
Analyze a Scene with an ESP32-CAM
Robot with an ESP32, Movement Sensor and three.js
ESP32-CAM with Capacitive Touch Buttons and OLED
Capacitive Soil Moisture Sensor with ESP32 (LoRa, Deep Sleep, MQTT)
sources.notes: Excellent ESP32-CAM and Raspberry Pi Zero focused projects — full textual tutorials with commands, code, hardware lists. Site is sparse (last entries 2022) but evergreen reference content (PocketSphinx voice control, OpenCV scene analysis, BlueALSA). Worth ingesting historical posts as Spanish translation backlog rather than relying on new cadence.

sources.name: Electronic Clinic
sample_titles_recent: This ESP32 Voice Recorder Has No Buttons and No Touch – Here's How It Works
Can a $5 ESP32 Mic Sound Like a Studio Microphone? I Built a $5 Studio Mic
Mastering the XIAO ESP32-C5: Dual-Band Wi-Fi 6 Power in a Tiny IoT Board
MaTouch ESP32-S3 Parallel TFT Display: Why You MUST See This 3.16" ST7701S Before Buying!
Engr. Shahzada Fahad ESP32 projects
sources.notes: Engr. Shahzada Fahad's blog — ESP32 / Arduino / IoT tutorials with full code, software setup (Arduino IDE board/library install), wiring, upload steps. Sample article ~35KB textual body inside <article>. Active weekly cadence, last post May 19, 2026. Some 'watch the video' references but text content is full-tutorial complete. Strong fit for MechatronicStore ESP32 + sensor catalog.

sources.name: Pi My Life Up
sample_titles_recent: Cup and Counter Demo now Available
Cup and Counter Dev Blog #2
Cup and Counter Dev Blog 1
Raspberry Pi Temperature Sensor
Pi Hole Setup
sources.notes: Emmet's RPi-focused tutorial site. Recent RSS shows dev-blog updates (~6KB textual) but archived tutorials are full-length (~22KB+, e.g. temperature sensor walkthrough with wiring, code, OS setup). Best to ingest /tutorials/ subpath rather than the news mix. Excellent fit for MechatronicStore Pi catalog (Pi 4/5/Pico, kits, screens, accessories).

sources.name: MagPi Magazine (Raspberry Pi Official)
sample_titles_recent: PiPower 5
ROOT Observer: a privacy-focused security camera
8BitDo 64 Controller review
Paper console
Take Back Your Cloud in Raspberry Pi Official Magazine 165
sources.notes: Official Raspberry Pi magazine. Many articles are excerpts pointing to PDF magazine download. Body length on free web articles is small (~8KB). Editorial mix: reviews, project showcases, magazine TOCs. Useful as RPi authority signal but expect to filter out paywalled stubs.

sources.name: Raspberry Pi News (Official)
sample_titles_recent: OVCS: Raspberry Pi–powered electric car
Powering coexistence: Raspberry Pi technology helping WWF protect wildlife in Pakistan
Search our documentation by meaning, not keywords
Maker Monday: Some of the best RP2350-based boards
AI Hat+ tutorial: object detection on Raspberry Pi 5
sources.notes: Official Raspberry Pi Foundation blog. Mix of product announcements, case studies (~50KB articles), and 'Maker Monday' roundups. Pi 5/Pico/RP2350 launches surface here first. High SEO authority, good for backlinks/topical coverage. Filter for tutorial-tagged posts ('Maker Monday', 'How-to', 'AI Hat+ tutorial') to skip pure PR.

sources.name: Robohub
sample_titles_recent: Table tennis robot defeats some of world's best players – why this has major implications for robotics
Robot Talk Episode 156 — Rugged robots for dangerous missions, with Gavin Kenneally
Developing active and flexible microrobots
How to teach the same skill to different robots
Robot Talk Episode 155 — Making aerial robots smarter, with Melissa Greeff
sources.notes: Non-profit robotics editorial hub, run by IFRR/IEEE-aligned contributors. Daily cadence, 75 items in feed. Full-text articles (~30KB) covering academic research, industry, podcast transcripts. Heavy on humanoids/cobots/swarm — aligns with Pablo's Robotics category definition (NOT Arduino DIY).

sources.name: 3D Printing Industry
sample_titles_recent: University of Illinois Team Is Engineering the Fix for the AI Heat Crisis
AMA: Healthcare 2026: How to Overcome the Red Tape Sabotaging Medical Implant Innovation
1st Special Forces Group Is Using 3D Printing to Redefine Army Readiness
ORNL Combines 3D Printing and High-Pressure Processing to Reshape Large-Scale Metal Part Production
sources.notes: Leading 3DP industry news outlet. Daily cadence, full articles (~58KB body). Skews B2B/industrial (metal AM, healthcare implants, defense) — less hobbyist FDM but valuable for MechatronicStore's printer/filament authority. Filter by category for consumer-FDM content.

sources.name: 3Dnatives (English)
sample_titles_recent: How TPM3D Is Using SLS PEBA to Scale 3D-Printed Footwear
Mastrex Expands Access to Metal AM With Affordable MX-Series LPBF Systems
A 3D-Printed Hydrogel Implant Could Treat High Blood Pressure Without Medication
incremental3d and The Challenges of 3D Printing with Concrete for Design and Architecture
sources.notes: Multi-language 3DP outlet, English feed verified. Daily cadence, ~17KB body articles. Mix of industrial AM + consumer-side reviews + how-to. Also has Spanish edition (3dnatives.com/es/feed/) — worth adding separately to hispano scope to skip translation costs.

sources.name: Prusa Blog (Original Prusa 3D Printers)
sample_titles_recent: Contest: CORE One Skins
Prusa CORE One INDX — Unmatched Toolchanger With 8 Nozzles — Orders Now Open!
Remote 1080p/24fps Camera Streaming For Everyone and New Prusa Connect Storage Upgrade Options
Contest: Upcycling NEW Prusament Spools
sources.notes: Official Prusa Research blog. Product launches, firmware/Connect updates, printing tutorials (~27KB body). MechatronicStore sells printers/filaments → strong vendor authority signal. Note: Pablo's catalog has Prusa-compatible accessories; align scoring weight with sales correlation.

sources.name: KiCad Blog
sample_titles_recent: KiCad 10.0.3 Release
KiCad 10.0.2 Release
KiCad Version 10.0.2 Release Candidate 1 Available
KiCad 9.0.9 Release
KiCad Version 9.0.9 Release Candidate 1 Available
sources.notes: Official KiCad project blog (CERN-incubated, Linux Foundation hosted). Heavy on release notes (~3KB short posts) but periodic deep-dive tutorials on PCB workflow. 151 items in feed = full archive. Pairs well with MechatronicStore PCB customers + electronics-design audience. Note: NOT a tutorial-first site — filter for 'how to' / 'tutorial' / 'guide' tagged posts only.

sources.name: SparkFun Tutorials
sample_titles_recent: What's the difference between the ZED-F9P and the ZED-X20P?
Build Your Own High-Concentration CO2 Detector
Qwiic & Easy DIY Ambient Light Indicator
Breadboard Power Supply Kit 5V/3.3V Quickstart Guide
Setup and Using MicroPython for Beginners
sources.notes: Atom feed at /feeds/tutorials (NOT /feed.xml). SparkFun's official tutorial library — peer to Adafruit Learn but with their own product ecosystem (Qwiic, RedBoard, SparkX). Full tutorials with hookup guides, code, library install. Body length ~20KB+ even on summary pages, more on /all subpath. Top-tier quality. Companion blog feed: https://learn.sparkfun.com/feeds/blog (20 items).

sources.name: Tindie Blog
sample_titles_recent: May 12th Update
More on the transition
Back up and running!
Classic Radio Shack Protoboards
DIY UPS System
sources.notes: Tindie's editorial blog (separate from www.tindie.com which is the marketplace). Showcases of seller projects — bytebeat synths, DIY UPS, PDP-1 replica, protoboards. ~14KB articles. Slower cadence post-2024 (acquisition transition phase) but evergreen DIY hardware coverage. Includes occasional hands-on reviews of small-batch maker hardware that maps to MechatronicStore's audience.

sources.name: LCSC Blog
sample_titles_recent: Hall Effect vs Reed Switch: Technical Selection Guide for Engineers
Circuit Calculator Basics: Voltage, Current & Resistance
How to Select the Right Temperature Sensor for Embedded and Industrial Designs
How to Select a MOSFET for Motor Control
Engineer's selection guides
sources.notes: LCSC (JLCPCB's component distributor) educational blog. RSS works from outside CN (feed returns ~192KB, 10 items, ~500-char descriptions). However the blog HTML host (blogs.lcsc.com) has a CN/internal-IP routing issue that blocks external curl — feed parser must rely on RSS body content + image URLs, not full-page fetches. Content is excellent: component selection guides (Hall vs Reed, MOSFET selection, temp sensor selection) which align perfectly with MechatronicStore's component/sensor catalog.

sources.name: EEVblog
sample_titles_recent: EEVblog 1748 — Dumpster iMac BONANZA!
EEVblog 1746 — Schottky vs PN Diodes & Measurement Traps
EEVblog 1746 — The 555 is 55 Years Old!
Mailbag teardowns
555 Timer anniversary articles
sources.notes: Dave Jones's EEVblog companion to YouTube. WARNING: most posts are SHORT show-notes with a video embed and 1-2 paragraphs (~25KB total page including chrome, actual textual content ~1-2KB). Educational topics (Schottky vs PN diodes, 555 timer history, measurement traps) DO have some textual context but the bulk of value is in the video. INGEST WITH CAUTION: many entries will be too thin for full-text translation. Could be useful as topic-signal/tagging source rather than full-article ingest. Pablo: decide whether to set min_text_chars filter to 5000+ to suppress show-note-only entries.

sources.name: Jeremy Blum (companion blog)
sample_titles_recent: Shaper BenchPilot
Shaper BenchPilot: The Reviews are In
Shaper Trace: Sketch to Vector in Seconds
Shaper Origin Gen2
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED. Jeremy Blum's site is a portfolio of his employer products (Shaper Tools) + book promo, NOT tutorials. Sample post 'Shaper BenchPilot' body: 'Here's a great video from one of our customers using BenchPilot' (~76 chars desc). Useless for text-blog ingest. His Arduino tutorials live in his book ('Exploring Arduino') and old YouTube content, not the blog feed.

sources.name: Contextual Electronics
sample_titles_recent: CEP015 — Finishing Prototypes with Zack Freedman
CEP014 — Moving to KiCad V6 with Jon Evans
CEP013 — Open Source Robotics with Kat Scott
Other Contextual Electronics Podcast episodes
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED. RSS structure works (10 items, 244-char descriptions) but the actual articles are PODCAST show-notes (timestamps + guest intros, ~3.6KB body). The site sells paid PCB-design courses; the free blog is just podcast episode pages. No textual tutorials in the feed. Chris Gammell's actual tutorials are gated behind contextualelectronics.com/course/ paid membership.

sources.name: Workshopshed
sample_titles_recent: Posts, Beams and Trusses
Footings and framing
Workshopshed 4
He He Helium
St Mungo's Mini Builds
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED. UK shed-DIY blog. Has an /electronics/ category but the RSS feed is dominated by carpentry/shed-renovation posts (beams, trusses, framing). Too thin on electronics tutorials to justify ingest noise. If category filter on /electronics/ subpath is added, could promote to qualifies=true with niche=general — but cadence in that subpath is sparse.

sources.name: Adafruit Industries Blog (blog.adafruit.com)
sample_titles_recent: The making of a kinetic sculpture #ArtTuesday
Designing an FPGA calculator from scratch
Pi-Hole Without the Pi
A Raspberry Pi AMA with a focus on industrial and embedded use
NEW PRODUCT — Machined Red Aluminum Servo Arm — 1.75" Long
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED to avoid DUPLICATION with adafruit-learn (already a confirmed source). blog.adafruit.com is the company news/product-launch feed (separate from learn.adafruit.com tutorials). Has 50 items/day cadence but content overlaps heavily with adafruit-learn. If ingested alongside, dedup would be heavy. Pablo: revisit if scoring shows learn-only is missing product-launch tagging value.

sources.name: DroneBot Workshop
sources.notes: FEED MISSING. Bill (DroneBot Workshop) runs an excellent companion blog to his YouTube channel — full Arduino/ESP32/RPi tutorials with code, BOM, wiring. BUT no RSS endpoint exposed: tested /feed/, /feed, /?feed=rss2, /rss, /blog/feed/, /tutorials/feed/, /category/tutorials/feed/, /news/feed/ — all 404. Sitemap exists at /sitemap_index.xml (200, well-formed) but is not RSS. Workarounds: (1) build a custom sitemap-poller adapter (DBW publishes weekly so polling /sitemap.xml hourly catches new posts), or (2) reach out via DBW's site form for an RSS endpoint. Recommend FUTURE-WORK rather than ingest now.

sources.name: Andreas Spiess
sources.notes: FEED UNREACHABLE. 'The Guy With Swiss Accent' YouTube channel. Domain andreasspiess.altervista.org returns connection error from outside (timeout/DNS) — Altervista host may be on its way to shutdown. His blog repos (github.com/SensorsIot) host code + writeups but no proper RSS. Recommend dropping in favor of his GitHub releases via the GH atom feed if depth-of-content matters.

sources.name: GreatScott!
sources.notes: FEED UNREACHABLE / DOMAIN DEAD. greatscottlab.com returns connection error. greatscott.io returns HTTP 402. His project files live on Patreon / GitHub but no text blog. Disqualified.

sources.name: Diode Gone Wild
sources.notes: FEED UNREACHABLE. diodegonewild.com domain doesn't resolve. Content lives YouTube-only (DiodeGoneWild teardowns). Disqualified.

sources.name: EngineerMade
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED. WordPress RSS returns valid XML structure but zero items (empty channel). Likely dormant site or feed misconfiguration. No content to ingest.

sources.name: Electronoobs
sources.notes: FEED MISSING. Excellent YouTube channel (electronics+drones+sensors) with companion site that has full tutorials in HTML — but /?feed=rss2 returns 200 with HTML body (homepage), no /feed/. Custom adapter (scrape /tutorials/ index pages) needed. FUTURE-WORK candidate.

sources.name: Internet of Home Things
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED. WordPress feed returns 251 bytes — basically empty. Homepage body is also tiny (43 chars stripped). Site looks abandoned/redirected. Not worth the integration cost.

sources.name: PCBWay Blog
sources.notes: FEED MISSING. PCBWay /blog/rss.xml returns HTTP 200 but body is HTML (not RSS). Tried /blog/rss.html, /blog/RSS.xml, /blog/rss.aspx — all return HTML. PCBWay sponsors many makers and has good content but no proper feed. Custom scraper of /blog/News/ would be needed. FUTURE-WORK.

sources.name: Hackster.io
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED for now. hackster.io/feed returns HTTP 200 but body is the login page (sign-in wall). Tested /news/feed.atom (404) and /feed.atom (404). Hackster has rich tutorial content but no public RSS. Would need authenticated scraping. FUTURE-WORK candidate (high-priority once auth pipeline exists).

sources.name: Maker.io (Digi-Key)
sources.notes: FEED MISSING. Digi-Key's maker-focused tutorial portal. Tested /feed, /rss — both unreachable. Excellent content (Arduino, ESP32, Raspberry Pi, sensors with Digi-Key parts) but no exposed RSS. FUTURE-WORK candidate (custom scraper).

sources.name: Elektor Magazine
sources.notes: DISQUALIFIED. /feed serves 1.8MB HTML response (homepage), not RSS. Tested /news.rss, /blog.rss, elektormagazine.com/rss — 404. Elektor's content is paywalled magazine — would not yield free-text articles even with feed.
